of source map comments, which are typical for transpiled TypeScript code.\n- No reports identify any suspicious code, hardcoded secrets, dynamic code execution, or malicious patterns.\n- The scores for malware and security risk are consistently zero or very low, aligning with the descriptions.\n- Confidence levels are high (mostly 1), indicating the reviewers agree on the benign nature.\n\n---\n\n### Confirming the code's actual content:\nThe original code:\n```js\n\"use strict\";\nObject.defineProperty(exports, \"__esModule\", { value: true });\nconst tslib_1 = require(\"tslib\");\ntslib_1.__exportStar(require(\"./common\"), exports);\n//# sourceMappingURL=index.js.map\n```\n\nThis code:\n- Uses strict mode.\n- Defines `__esModule` property.\n- Imports `tslib` for helper functions.\n- Calls `__exportStar` to re-export all exports from `./common`.\n- Contains a source map comment.\n\n**No dynamic code, no hardcoded secrets, no suspicious behavior.** It’s a standard module index file.\n\n---\n\n### Review of each report:\n\n**Report 1:**  \n- **Purpose & sources:** Accurate.  \n- **Analysis & conclusion:** Correctly identifies this as a typical re-export file, no malicious activity.  \n- **Scores:** Malware and security risk are 0, which is appropriate.  \n- **Verdict:** No change needed.\n\n**Report 2:**  \n- Same as Report 1, with similar
